name: Azure OpenAI Service API
what_it_does: Azure OpenAI Service REST API — Microsoft-hosted OpenAI models (GPT-4o, GPT-4, embeddings, DALL-E, Whisper) with enterprise compliance, private networking, content filtering, and Azure IAM integration, enabling agents to use OpenAI models within Azure's compliance boundary.
use_cases: Enterprise agents running GPT-4o within Azure compliance boundary — same OpenAI API surface but with HIPAA BAA, FedRAMP, and private VNet access for regulated industries
Embeddings at scale — agents using Azure OpenAI embedding models to generate vectors for Azure AI Search integration, keeping data within Azure tenant
Image generation — agents using DALL-E 3 via Azure OpenAI to generate images with content policy controls configured per enterprise requirements
Speech transcription — agents using Whisper via Azure OpenAI to transcribe audio with data staying within Azure tenant boundary
Fine-tuned model deployment — agents deploying fine-tuned GPT-3.5/4 models on Azure with dedicated capacity for consistent latency
not_for: Immediate access to newest OpenAI models — Azure OpenAI typically lags OpenAI's direct API by weeks to months for new model availability
Pay-as-you-go without Azure account — Azure OpenAI requires Azure subscription and is quota-gated; direct OpenAI API is faster to start
Non-enterprise use cases — the setup overhead (Azure subscription, resource creation, model deployment) isn't justified for personal or small projects
best_when: Your enterprise requires data residency within Azure, HIPAA/FedRAMP compliance, private network access to OpenAI models, or needs Azure Active Directory-based access control for AI model usage.
avoid_when: You want immediate access to the latest OpenAI models or need a simple API key to get started — direct OpenAI API is faster and simpler for non-enterprise use.

auth.notes: API key from Azure Portal (per-resource). Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) OAuth2 bearer token for managed identity / service principal auth — preferred for production. Endpoint URL includes Azure subscription, resource group, resource name, and deployment name. Different endpoint per model deployment.

pricing.paid_tiers: Same token pricing as OpenAI: GPT-4o: $2.50/$10 per 1M input/output tokens; GPT-4o-mini: $0.15/$0.60 per 1M tokens; Embeddings: $0.02/1M tokens; PTU (Provisioned) for committed throughput
pricing.notes: Pay-per-token with same pricing as direct OpenAI API. Provisioned Throughput Units (PTU) for guaranteed capacity at committed usage. No free tier — Azure subscription with billing required. Content filtering adds no cost.

performance.rate_limits: Per-deployment: tokens per minute (TPM) and requests per minute (RPM) quotas; default varies by model and region; quota increase requests via Azure Portal

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Same error format as OpenAI API. Rate limit errors include retry-after header. Content filter rejections have specific error codes with filter category details. Azure-specific errors for quota and resource issues are clear.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Stateless inference — same request produces equivalent completion. No client-side idempotency mechanism. Batch API (async batch jobs) tracks by batch ID. Agents should deduplicate at application level.
known_agent_gotchas: Each model requires a separate deployment — agents cannot call 'gpt-4o' by name; must use the deployment name configured in Azure Portal, which may differ across environments
Model quota is per-region per-subscription — agents needing higher TPM must request quota increases per region, or implement cross-region load balancing with different Azure resources

Content filters are enabled by default and configured at resource level — agents hitting content filter rejections see error code 'content_filter' and must adjust prompts or request filter configuration changes
Azure OpenAI endpoint URLs are resource-specific (https://{resource}.openai.azure.com) — hardcoded URLs break when moving between environments; use environment variables for endpoint configuration
Model version availability differs from OpenAI — gpt-4-turbo-preview on Azure may refer to a different snapshot than on OpenAI direct; always pin to specific model version strings

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure AD managed identity (no credentials in code). Private endpoints for VNet isolation. Customer Managed Keys (BYOK) for data encryption. HIPAA BAA, FedRAMP High authorized. Microsoft abuse monitoring. Content filtering configurable. Prompts not used for training.
